lines.target: I've just been over the figures with the accountant and he says we'll have to be careful next year.
lines.base: Acabo de examinar las cifras con el contable y de acuerdo con lo que dice nosotros deberíamos tener mucho cuidado el año que viene.

lines.target: Turnover is up three-and-a-half percent (3½ %) and return on investment is steady,
lines.base: El volumen de negocio se eleva hasta el tres y medio porciento (3,5 %) y el beneficio sobre la inversión es estable

lines.target: but our overheads are also up — by four point two percent (4.2 %).
lines.base: pero nuestros gastos generales son también altos y se elevan hasta 4,2 %.

lines.target: Our interest repayments on the bank loan come to two hundred and sixteen pounds (£216) a month
lines.base: Nuestros reembolsos de intereses sobre los préstamos bancarios alcanzan las doscientas dieciséis libras (216 £) al mes

lines.target: and investment on R & D is constant at a thousand pounds (£1,000) per quarter.
lines.base: y la inversión en I+D (Investigación y Desarrollo) es constante [y alcanza] las mil libras (1.000 £) por trimestre.

lines.target: One thousand! Isn't that a little high?
lines.base: ¡Mil libras! ¿No es un poco alto? — Los nuevos productos son la vida de la industria, muchacho.

lines.target: Well, if we want to cut back, we can start by looking at bonuses. Look at this: two thousand four hundred and sixteen pounds (£2,416) productivity bonus to Miller.
lines.base: Bien, si queremos reducir, pudiéramos empezar por las primas. Fíjate en esto: dos mil cuatrocientas dieciséis libras (2.416 £) de prima de productividad para Miller.

lines.target: Well, two thousand pounds is a lot of money, but Miller's the product manager for the RX 214
lines.base: Bueno, dos mil libras es mucho dinero, pero Miller es el jefe de producto del RX 214

lines.target: and look how successful that's been.
lines.base: y considera el éxito que eso ha tenido. — ¿Ah, te parece? Observa las cifras:

lines.target: sales up by nought point three percent (0.3 %) for the year. Not exactly what you'd call record-breaking...
lines.base: las ventas han subido un cero punto tres por ciento (0,3 %) durante el año. No es lo que llamaríamos exactamente un nuevo récord...

lines.target: and look at these two: a thousand pounds to Cookson — and one thousand four hundred to Mathey.
lines.base: y observa estas dos: mil libras para Cookson — y mil cuatrocientas libras para Mathey.

lines.target: Yes, I suppose we'll have to cut back on generosity.
lines.base: Sí, supongo que tendremos que reducir la generosidad.

lines.target: This used to be a family business, my boy, but now we're in a billion-dollar industry, and money's tight.
lines.base: Esto en otro tiempo era un negocio familiar, hijo, pero ahora somos una industria de un billón de dólares, y el dinero se hace escaso.

lines.target: Well, if it comes to that, Dad, you're not exactly hard up. — I worked my way up from the bottom.
lines.base: Bueno, con respecto a eso, papá, tú no estás verdaderamente «pelado». — Empecé desde abajo.

lines.target: When I started, I didn't have two ha'pennies to rub together. — Yes, yes I know.
lines.base: Cuando empecé, no tenía ni siquiera dos duros. — Sí, sí, lo sé. ¿Cuándo es tu próxima reunión con el banco?

lines.target: On the thirteenth. I'm going to ask the manager to reschedule the repayment of the last loan.
lines.base: El día trece, voy a pedirle al director que me intente espaciar los pagos del último préstamo.

lines.target: Oh Lord! I hope you're not superstitious. Your meeting's on Friday the thirteenth!
lines.base: ¡Dios mío! Espero que no seas supersticioso. ¡Tu reunión es el viernes trece!

lines.target: On the contrary. Hundreds of people choose Friday the thirteenth. It's a lucky day.
lines.base: Por el contrario, cientos de personas eligen el viernes trece. ¡Es un día que trae suerte!

lines.target: Oh, and I must call Maxwell at home — the warehouse switchboard shuts down at the weekend. Have you got his number to hand?
lines.base: Oh, y debo llamar a Maxwell a su casa — la centralita del almacén se cierra el fin de semana. ¿Tienes su número a mano?

lines.target: Yes, it's seven-oh-six, three-one-seven two (706 3172). The dialling code's oh-two-one-two .
lines.base: Sí, es el siete cero seis, tres uno siete dos (706 3172). El prefijo es el cero dos uno dos (0212).

lines.target: Thanks, son. Oh, by the way, could you lend me a couple of quid for a taxi?
lines.base: Gracias, hijo. Oh, a propósito, ¿me prestas un par de libras para un taxi?

lines.target: Rockefeller was asked how he became a millionaire. — One day, I found a dime on the ground. I used it to buy an apple.
lines.base: Se pidió a Rockefeller que comentara cómo se había convertido en millonario. — Un día, me encontré diez centavos en el suelo, los utilicé para comprar una manzana.

lines.target: Then I sold the apple for a quarter. I bought two apples for that, and then sold them at a profit.
lines.base: Luego vendí la manzana por veinticinco centavos, compré dos manzanas con esa cantidad y luego las vendí con beneficio.

lines.target: I would have continued like that, but my father died and I inherited his fortune.
lines.base: Debía haber continuado así, pero mi padre murió y heredé su fortuna.

gap_candidates.target: I've just been over the figures with the accountant and he says we'll have to be careful next year.
Turnover is up three-and-a-half percent (3½ %) and return on investment is steady,
but our overheads are also up — by four point two percent (4.2 %).
Our interest repayments on the bank loan come to two hundred and sixteen pounds (£216) a month
and investment on R & D is constant at a thousand pounds (£1,000) per quarter.
One thousand! Isn't that a little high?
Well, if we want to cut back, we can start by looking at bonuses. Look at this: two thousand four hundred and sixteen pounds (£2,416) productivity bonus to Miller.
Well, two thousand pounds is a lot of money, but Miller's the product manager for the RX 214
and look how successful that's been.
sales up by nought point three percent (0.3 %) for the year. Not exactly what you'd call record-breaking...
and look at these two: a thousand pounds to Cookson — and one thousand four hundred to Mathey.
Yes, I suppose we'll have to cut back on generosity.
This used to be a family business, my boy, but now we're in a billion-dollar industry, and money's tight.
Well, if it comes to that, Dad, you're not exactly hard up. — I worked my way up from the bottom.
When I started, I didn't have two ha'pennies to rub together. — Yes, yes I know.
On the thirteenth. I'm going to ask the manager to reschedule the repayment of the last loan.
Oh Lord! I hope you're not superstitious. Your meeting's on Friday the thirteenth!
On the contrary. Hundreds of people choose Friday the thirteenth. It's a lucky day.
Oh, and I must call Maxwell at home — the warehouse switchboard shuts down at the weekend. Have you got his number to hand?
Yes, it's seven-oh-six, three-one-seven two (706 3172). The dialling code's oh-two-one-two (0212).
Thanks, son. Oh, by the way, could you lend me a couple of quid for a taxi?
Rockefeller was asked how he became a millionaire. — One day, I found a dime on the ground. I used it to buy an apple.
Then I sold the apple for a quarter. I bought two apples for that, and then sold them at a profit.
I would have continued like that, but my father died and I inherited his fortune.
